data.title: AITX partner orders two ROSA units to add AI-driven detection-to-guard response at retail
data.description:
Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions announced a channel partner ordered two ROSA devices powered by SARA to extend detection-to-guard response at a retail property.

Key Highlights:

  • Channel partner ordered two ROSA security devices running SARA to complete detection-to-guard response architecture at a retail site.
  • Deployment shifts retail security from continuous on-site guards to edge-based autonomous detection with human escalation only when needed.
  • Company says solution aims to improve oversight and lower operating costs amid rising guard labor expenses.
  • RAD completed SOC 2 Type 2 audit, reinforcing internal controls for handling customer data and enterprise/government credibility.
  • RAD highlights a prospective pipeline including over 35 Fortune 500 companies and will demo solutions at ISC West 2026.

Original SEC Filing: Artificial Intelligence Technology Solutions Inc. [ AITX ] - 8-K - Mar. 04, 2026

data.title: Gold gains as Middle East conflict revives safe-haven bid
data.description:
Key points:
  • Gold rises above 2%, silver up over 5%
  • Platinum up 5%, Palladium up over 3%
  • Oil, gas prices surge as Middle East energy shipments stall

By Pablo Sinha

Gold prices climbed 2% on Wednesday, rebounding from their lowest in more than a week reached in the previous session, as the dollar took a breather and mounting tensions in the Middle East drove investors toward safe havens.

Spot gold gained 2.2% to $5,198.58 per ounce by 1017 GMT, after falling more than 4% on Tuesday.

U.S. gold futures for April delivery added 1.7% to $5,211.20.

The U.S. dollar fell 0.2%, making greenback-priced gold more affordable for buyers using other currencies.

"After the past few days of position unwinds and dollar strength, markets are back to a more typical macro risk-off stance, with silver higher too. A pause in the rise of the dollar and Treasury yields helps with their opportunity costs," said Jamie Dutta, market analyst at Nemo.money.

"Gold and silver's safe-haven characteristics can shine again."

Gold's appeal as it draws support from the widening conflict in the Middle East is expected to remain intact even if some investors have favored the dollar as their preferred safe-haven, traders and analysts said on Tuesday.

Spot silver advanced 5.3% to $86.39 per ounce, after falling more than 8% in the last session.

U.S. forces continued round-the-clock assaults on Iran, and Israel mounted a "broad wave" of strikes targeting Iranian missile sites and air defense systems.

Asian stocks tanked as investors dumped crowded bets on chipmakers on worries that the widening Middle East war would drive an oil shock, accelerating inflation and delaying interest rate cuts.

Markets see the U.S. Federal Reserve holding rates at its two-day meeting later this month. (FEDWATCH)

"If the military campaign prolongs or expands across the region, safe-haven demand could continue to support gold above the $5,000/oz level and potentially open the door for a retest of the recent highs," said Linh Tran, senior market analyst at XS.com.

Spot platinum added 5.1% to $2,189.68 an ounce. The global platinum market is forecast to post a fourth consecutive year of deficit in 2026 at 240,000 troy ounces, the World Platinum Investment Council said. Palladium gained 3.6% to $1,705.71.

data.title: Hormuz reopening is not solely in Trump’s gift
data.description:
By Yawen Chen

The US president thinks tanker insurance and Navy escorts will restart Strait flows. But his fleets are targets, escorts lack ships, and precedent suggests shipping volumes don’t instantly recover from these sorts of shocks. Unless hostilities end soon, $100 oil looks likely.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow Yawen Chen on Bluesky and LinkedIn.

CONTEXT NEWS

The U.S. Navy could begin escorting oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if necessary, President Donald Trump said on March 3, adding he had ordered the U.S. International Development Finance Corporation (DFC) to provide political risk insurance and financial guarantees for maritime trade in the Gulf.

The DFC said on March 3 it is ready to mobilize its Political Risk Insurance and Guaranty products to stabilize international commerce and support American and allied businesses operating in the Middle East during this period of conflict with the Iranian regime. Brent crude rose 3.3% to $84 a barrel by 0659 GMT on March 4, after closing on March 3 at its highest since January 2025.

data.title: GE Shipping expands fleet with new vessel buy
data.description:
https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/stocks/great-eastern-shipping-company-ltd-share-price/7I03QD/Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd (GE Shipping) has contracted to acquire a secondhand Kamsarmax dry bulk carrier as part of its ongoing fleet expansion strategy.

In an exchange filing on March 4, 2026, the company said it has agreed to purchase a 2014 Japanese-built Kamsarmax vessel with a capacity of approximately 81,094 deadweight tonnes (dwt). The vessel is expected to join the company’s fleet by the first quarter of FY27.

GE Shipping said the purchase aligns with its strategy to expand and modernise its fleet.

Following this acquisition, the company’s owned fleet stands at 41 vessels, comprising 27 tankers, including five crude tankers, 17 product tankers and five LPG carriers, and 14 dry bulk carriers, which include two Capesize, nine Kamsarmax, one Ultramax and two Supramax vessels. The total fleet capacity aggregates to about 3.25 million dwt. The company’s current capacity utilisation is close to 100%.

Separately, GE Shipping has contracted to sell its Very Large Gas Carrier (VLGC) Jag Vishnu. The transaction is expected to be completed in the fourth quarter of FY26.

Q3 Results

GE Shipping reported a strong financial performance for the third quarter of FY26, with net profit rising 36.9% year-on-year to ₹812 crore, compared with ₹593 crore in the corresponding quarter last year.

Revenue for the quarter increased 17.6% year-on-year to ₹1,454 crore. Earnings before interest, tax, depreciation and amortisation (EBITDA) surged 36.7% to ₹835.2 crore from ₹611 crore a year ago.

Operating margin expanded sharply to 57.4% from 49.4% in the year-ago period, supported by firm freight rates and operating leverage.

The company’s board also declared a third interim dividend of ₹9 per equity share for FY26.

Shares of https://www.cnbctv18.com/market/stocks/great-eastern-shipping-company-ltd-share-price/7I03QD/Great Eastern Shipping Company Ltd ended higher on Wednesday, March 4, by 2.14% at ₹1354.80 on the NSE.

data.title: Eurozone Unemployment Rate Falls
data.description:
BRUSSELS/FRANKFURT/PARIS (dpa-AFX) - The euro area unemployment rate dropped slightly in January, Eurostat reported Wednesday.

The jobless rate fell to seasonally adjusted 6.1 percent from 6.2 percent in December. The rate was forecast to remain unchanged at 6.2 percent.

The number of unemployed people decreased by 184,000 from December. Compared to last year, unemployment fell 273,000 to 10.77 million.

There were 2.352 million young unemployed persons in January, which was down by 27,000 from December. The youth unemployment rate decreased to 14.8 percent from 15.0 percent in December.

Data showed that the unemployment rate in the EU27 came in at 5.8 percent compared to 5.9 percent in December.

data.title: National Vision 2025 10-K: $1.99B Revenue, $0.37 Diluted EPS
data.description:
National Vision reported fiscal 2025 net revenue of $1,987.5 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.37, reflecting a return to profitability after the prior-year loss. The company cited revenue growth driven by pricing, product mix and exam growth while adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations was $0.80 on a non-GAAP basis. Net income for the year was $29.6 million, compared with a loss in the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total net revenue: $1,987.5 million, up $164.2 million (9.0%) versus prior year (includes a 53-week year that added $35.6 million).
  • Gross profit: $1,168.0 million (reported as total net revenue minus total costs applicable to revenue).
  • Income (loss) from operations: $58.8 million (improved from a $(10.4) million loss in the prior year).
  • Net income: $29.6 million (prior year: $(28.5) million); discontinued operations contribution was $0 in 2025.
  • Diluted net income per share: $0.37; Adjusted diluted EPS from continuing operations (non-GAAP): $0.80. Weighted diluted shares: 80.576 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth attributed to pricing and product mix improvements and increased eye exam volumes.
  • Channel & brand performance: Owned store portfolio totaled roughly 1,250 locations with America’s Best delivering 6.3% comparable-store growth.
  • Customer mix & managed care: Managed-care sales represented approximately 42% of revenue; a stronger managed-care cohort helped raise ticket value.
  • Operational modernization: Rolled out remote telemedicine services in over 800 locations and implemented in-store technology and brand refreshes during 2025.
  • Store expansion & capacity: Opened 33 new stores in 2025 and is targeting about 30–35 new America’s Best stores in 2026 to expand exam capacity.

Original SEC Filing: National Vision Holdings, Inc. [ EYE ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Kontoor 2025 10-K: $3,152.5M Revenue, $4.05 EPS
data.description:
Kontoor reported fiscal 2025 net revenues of $3,152.5 million and diluted EPS of $4.05, reflecting a 21% revenue increase driven by the Helly Hansen acquisition and a $0.23 EPS contribution from that business. Gross margin improved to $1,423.4 million (45.2% of revenues), while operating income of $336.8 million and net income of $227.5 million were pressured by acquisition/integration, restructuring charges and a higher effective tax rate. The results include benefits from Project Jeanius and a ~2% tailwind from a 53rd week.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenues: $3,152.5 million, up 21% versus 2024 (includes $475.5 million from Helly Hansen and ~2% benefit from a 53rd week).
  • Gross margin (net revenues less cost of goods sold): $1,423.4 million, 45.2% of revenues, up 70 basis points (attributed to mix, Helly Hansen and Project Jeanius).
  • Operating income: $336.8 million, 10.7% of revenues, down 2% versus 2024 (includes acquisition/integration and restructuring charges).
  • Net income: $227.5 million, decreased 7% versus 2024 (impacted by higher effective tax rate and after-tax charges).
  • Diluted earnings per share: $4.05, down from $4.36 in 2024; included a $0.23 contribution from Helly Hansen and a $1.54 after-tax charge impact.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Growth was largely driven by the integration of Helly Hansen and organic Wrangler momentum.
  • Channel performance: Direct-to-consumer sales rose 49%; international wholesale increased 65%; U.S. wholesale grew modestly at 7%.
  • Brand and portfolio: The additions of Helly Hansen and Musto expanded Kontoor's premium outdoor and workwear scale, diversifying the portfolio.
  • Operational transformation: Project Jeanius continued, including plant closures, supply-chain diversification and cost optimization initiatives.
  • Margin and cost actions: Gross margin gains were driven by favorable mix and benefits from Project Jeanius despite ongoing restructuring, tariff and product cost pressures.

Original SEC Filing: Kontoor Brands, Inc. [ KTB ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Black Rock Coffee Bar posts $200.3M revenue, $(0.00) EPS — 10-K summary
data.description:
Black Rock Coffee Bar reported total revenue of $200.3 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, a 24.5% increase year-over-year driven by store growth and same-store sales gains. The company recorded a net loss of $16.539 million (net loss per share reported as ($0.00) on 17,021,377 weighted-average shares) and operating income declined to $0.901 million, reflecting margin pressure despite higher revenue. Store-level profitability improved, with Store-Level Profit (presented as gross profit) rising to $58.492 million, supporting unit economics as the chain scales.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $200.321 million, up 24.5% year-over-year to Dec 31, 2025.
  • Gross Profit (Store-Level Profit): $58.492 million; Store-Level Profit Margin 29.2% (up from 27.9%).
  • Operating Income (Income from operations): $0.901 million; operating margin 0.4%, down from $6.033 million (3.7%) prior year.
  • Net loss (Net Income): $(16.539) million; net loss margin (8.3%) versus $(7.187) million prior year.
  • Net loss per share (Net loss per share of Class A common stock): ($0.00), based on 17,021,377 weighted-average shares.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue drivers: Growth driven by store expansion and a 10.1% same-store sales increase composed of traffic (+6.7%) and menu price/mix (+4.5%).
  • Store expansion: Opened 32 net new drive-thru focused stores, bringing the total to 181 locations and achieving more than 20% new unit growth target.
  • Unit economics: Store-Level Profit Margin improved to 29.2% from 27.9% due to operational discipline and scale efficiencies.
  • Operational capabilities: Approximately 73% of locations include lobbies; investments made in the digital platform, production facilities, and corporate buildout to support continued growth.

Original SEC Filing: Black Rock Coffee Bar, Inc. [ BRCB ] - 10-K - Mar. 04, 2026

data.title: 9000 Plus Americans Returned From The Middle East: State Dept.
data.description:
JERUSALEM (dpa-AFX) - U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio has announced that more than 9000 American citizens have safely returned from the Middle East, including more than 300 from Israel, since the start of the war.

'We have about 1,500 Americans that are requesting assistance with departure. We have identified and continued to identify charter flights, military flight options, and expanded commercial flight options, meaning working with the airlines to send bigger airplanes with more seats, and a combination of those three things,' he said at a news conference Tuesday.

'The impediment we're facing now, in many cases - we've had a couple instances in which we have planes in the air and on the way, and unfortunately the airspace gets closed and they have to turn back around. So we're working through those challenges,' he told reporters.

In a message to Americans who are in the Middle East and in need of assistance, Rubio said that it is important to 'put both the website and the phone numbers on your screens, because we need to know where you are'. 'We need to have contact information for Americans that need assistance. They have to register with us, because as these options begin to open up, we have to be able to call you and know where you're staying so we can get this information to you and coordinate appropriately'.

He added that stranded Anericans will be evacuated using a variety of methods: 'charter flights, military flights and transports, expanded commercial opportunities, and in some cases land routes that will allow them to go to neighboring countries who might have open airports at that point'.

Separately, the U.S. Department of State said it is facilitating charter flights from the United Arab Emirates, Saudi Arabia, and Jordan for American citizens, and will continue to secure additional capacity as security conditions allow.

Commercial aviation options remain available in Saudi Arabia, UAE, Oman, and Egypt, and the Department is actively helping American citizens book those tickets. For those in countries lacking commercial aviation availability, the Department is facilitating travel to third countries as conditions allow. That includes increasing ground transportation options for American citizens wishing to leave Israel.

The State Department said it has answered calls from nearly 3000 American citizens in the Middle East and continues to provide travel options for those wishing to leave. The Department will also waive any statutory requirement for American citizens to reimburse the government for travel expenses.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Extreme strength in energy, but insiders sell - SentimenTrader
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 extends gains, up 1.5%
  • Broad gains, energy down
  • Gas prices turn negative
  • Madrid shrugs off Trump threat
  • Wall Street futures turn positive

EXTREME STRENGTH IN THE ENERGY SECTOR, BUT INSIDERS SELL - SENTIMENTRADER

The energy sector is on an extraordinary run, with every stock in the XLE ETF trading simultaneously above its 10-, 50- and 200-day moving averages – a signal triggered only 22 times in the past quarter of a century, according to research from SentimenTrader.

Such broad strength normally points to heavy capital inflows and powerful short‑term momentum, it notes, adding this clean sweep also aligns with the sector’s strongest seasonal window: March and April have historically delivered the best returns for energy shares.

But this year’s rally looks highly front‑loaded. XLE is up almost 28% in the first 40 trading days, far above the usual 2–4% gain for this period. That raises the risk that seasonal tailwinds may already have been priced in. At the same time, insider trading trends and internal momentum gauges are flashing caution.

Past episodes of similarly extreme breadth have often coincided with choppier conditions for the broader market and sharp sector divergence. Energy has tended to hold up, but growth sectors like communications have usually lagged, SentimenTrader adds, noting that this extreme crowding in real assets usually mirrors macro inflation-hedging trades.

"Historical probabilities tend to show that when an extremely strong momentum trend meets smart money's profit-taking and retreat, the market usually digests this divergence with sharp, wide-ranging volatility," it says.

data.title: CrowdStrike Posts Record ARR -- 2027 Outlook Calls for 24% Growth
data.description:
CrowdStrike Holdings outlined double-digit growth targets for fiscal 2027 after reporting stronger fourth-quarter results, driven by demand for its artificial intelligence-based cybersecurity platform.

For fiscal 2027, the company expects annual recurring revenue of $6.47 billion to $6.52 billion, representing growth of 23% to 24%. Total revenue is projected at $5.87 billion to $5.93 billion, up 22% to 23%, with non-GAAP earnings per share of $4.78 to $4.90. First-quarter revenue is forecast at $1.36 billion to $1.36 billion, with adjusted EPS of $1.06 to $1.07.

In the fourth quarter, revenue rose to $1.31 billion, including $1.24 billion from subscriptions. Non-GAAP net income was $289 million, or $1.12 per diluted share. Annual recurring revenue reached $5.25 billion at year-end, up 24% from a year earlier, while net new ARR totaled $331 million in the quarter.

The company cited continued adoption of its Falcon Flex subscription model and growth in cloud, identity and next-generation security information and event management, or SIEM, which analyzes security data in real time.

data.title: Oil Prices Extend Rally; S&P Futures Inch Up — WSJ
data.description:
By Chelsey Dulaney

U.S. and European markets stabilized as the Middle East conflict entered its fifth day, while a selloff in Asian stocks deepened.

Brent-crude prices climbed nearly 3% to top $83 a barrel, a smaller rise than the surges of recent days. President Trump said Tuesday the U.S. might escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz, a choke point for global energy flows that has come to a virtual standstill.

Oil prices are up about 15% this week so far, while natural-gas prices have soared in Europe and Asia.

Global stocks have been rocked by the widening Middle East fighting, which is snarling shipping and threatening to push inflation higher.

The selloff has hit overseas markets harder; as a net energy exporter, the U.S. is seen as more insulated than Asia and Europe to flow disruptions. South Korea's Kospi index tumbled a record 12% on Wednesday. Dubai's stock index fell nearly 5% as it reopened from a two-day trading halt due to the attacks.

Bitcoin rebounded on Wednesday after sliding earlier in the week.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Xponential Fitness reports $314.9M revenue, $(1.47) EPS — FY10-K summary
data.description:
Xponential reported total revenue of $314.9 million for the year, a decline of $5.5 million (1.7%) versus the prior year driven primarily by lower equipment and merchandise sales. The company recorded a net loss of $53.7 million, or $(1.47) per Class A share (basic and diluted), an improvement from the prior-year net loss of $98.7 million. Operating income improved to $19.8 million, reflecting lower selling, general & administrative expenses and reduced impairments versus the prior year.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue, net: $314.9 million, down $5.5 million (1.7%) year-over-year, primarily from lower equipment & merchandise sales.
  • Gross Profit: $272.5 million (implied by reported total revenue less costs of product revenue of $42.4 million), reflecting improved margin on product sales.
  • Operating income (loss): $19.8 million, improved from $(53.6) million in the prior year, benefiting from lower SG&A and reduced impairments.
  • Net loss: $(53.7) million, improved from $(98.7) million in the prior year after higher other expense (including debt extinguishment) impacts.
  • Net loss per share of Class A common stock (Basic and Diluted): $(1.47).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix: Franchise revenue increased 10% year-over-year to $192.6 million, offsetting declines in equipment and merchandise that kept total revenue roughly flat.
  • Studio footprint & AUV: Global studios grew to 3,097 locations with 341 openings during the period; North America last twelve months average unit volume (AUV) rose to $695, supporting recurring royalty streams.
  • Portfolio focus: The company divested CycleBar, Rumble, and Lindora to concentrate resources on core Pilates, barre, stretch, and yoga brands and to boost operational focus.
  • Cost actions & restructuring: Ongoing restructuring includes company-owned studio exits and lease negotiations targeting $13.5–$15.5 million in annualized savings, with remaining actions extending into 2026.
  • Channel & retail strategy: Retail merchandising was outsourced to Fit Commerce; the company sold $4.5 million of inventory and secured an approximately $50 million commission arrangement over five years.

Original SEC Filing: Xponential Fitness, Inc. [ XPOF ] - 10-K - Mar. 03, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Cricut 2025 10-K: Revenue $708.8M, EPS $0.35
data.description:
Cricut reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $708.8 million and diluted earnings per share of $0.35, with revenue roughly flat year‑over‑year and EPS up from $0.29 as net income rose to $76.7 million. The company cited a shift toward higher‑margin Platform revenue and improved product gross margins as drivers of profit growth. Investments in R&D and marketing increased to support product roadmaps and international expansion.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $708.8 million, a 1% decrease from $712.5 million in 2024.
  • Gross profit: $390.4 million, an 11% increase year over year driven by Platform margin improvement and lower inventory impairments.
  • Income from operations: $96.0 million, up from $76.1 million in 2024, reflecting higher gross profit offset by increased R&D and marketing expenses.
  • Net income: $76.7 million, a 22% increase from $62.8 million in 2024; effective tax rate approximately 28.9%.
  • Earnings per share, diluted: $0.35 (basic EPS $0.36), up from diluted EPS of $0.29 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: Platform revenue grew 5% year over year to $327.4 million, while product sales declined, contributing to the slight overall revenue decrease.
  • Channel transformation: Online channels increased to 69% of revenue in 2025 versus 62% in 2023, indicating continued migration from brick‑and‑mortar to digital sales.
  • User and monetization trends: Active Users remained approximately 5.9 million; Paid Subscribers grew 4% to 3.09 million, lifting Platform ARPU to $55.77.
  • Margin and inventory recovery: Product gross margin improved to 26% from 19%, attributed to lower inventory impairments and reduced procurement costs.
  • Operational investments and expansion: Increased R&D and marketing expenditures to support the product roadmap, international expansion, and content localization efforts.

Original SEC Filing: Cricut, Inc. [ CRCT ] - 10-K - Mar. 03, 2026

Disclaimer
